query: ECB TIBER-EU framework DORA updated framework publication date cyber resilience 2025

{"query":"ECB TIBER-EU framework DORA updated framework publication date cyber resilience 2025","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1565,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.ecb.europa.eu/press/intro/news/html/ecb.mipnews250211.en.html","title":"TIBER-EU Framework updated to align with DORA","content":"Several updates were introduced in the framework to incorporate regulatory requirements and align with other measures set out in DORA.
The updated framework and the corresponding guidance documents are available on this webpage, where you can also find out more about the framework itself.
A short ECB paper published in September 2024 also outlines how the TIBER-EU framework can help competent authorities and financial entities fulfil DORA TLPT requirements."},{"url":"https://deepstrike.io/blog/tiber-eu-framework-explained","title":"TIBER-EU Explained: 2025 Framework & DORA TLPT","content":"4 days ago — Learn how the 2025 TIBER-EU framework works, including its phases, roles, safety controls, attestation, and alignment with DORA TLPT.
The January 2025 framework is aligned with threat-led penetration testing under DORA, but for a mandatory test, DORA and Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2025/1190 define the binding requirements and take precedence.
Process steps and deliverables were aligned with the DORA regulatory technical standards for TLPT.
The ECB Banking Supervision guide describes it as detailed, non-legally binding operational guidance: the “how” of a threat-led test.
Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2025/1190 supplies the regulatory technical standards on identification, scope, methodology, testers, closure, remediation and cooperation.
TIBER-EU explains how to run a high-quality threat-led test; DORA and its RTS determine binding TLPT obligations for designated financial entities.
Once the TIBER Authority has approved the TSR and remediation plan, it issues and signs the attestation.
It is the ECB's European framework for controlled, bespoke, intelligence-led red-team testing.
He writes about penetration testing, red teaming and operational resilience."},{"url":"https://www.cybrops.io/posts/tiber-eu-framework-2025-update","title":"TIBER-EU Framework 2025 update - CybrOps","content":"TIBER-EU Framework 2025 update
From January 17, 2025, financial institutions, other than microenterprises and institutions mentioned in DORA Article 16(1)*, (hereinafter “in-scope financial institutions” or “entities”) must follow new, heightened Threat-Led Penetration Testing (TLPT) rules under the new regulation on operational resilience.
The framework is now completely aligned with DORA's TLPT requirements, enhancing the overall cyber resilience of financial institutions across the EU.
The TIBER-EU framework has updated key terms to align with DORA, ensuring consistency across different EU regulations.
In TIBER-EU 2025, Purple Teaming is now a mandatory phase of TLPT."},{"url":"https://www.ecb.europa.eu/paym/cyber-resilience/tiber-eu/html/index.en.html","title":"What is TIBER-EU?","content":"TIBER-EU is a European framework for threat intelligence-based ethical red-teaming.
Instead the test is intended to reveal the strengths and weaknesses of the cyber resilience measures put in place by the tested entity, with a focus on the learning effect of the test, and to enable the entity to reach a higher level of cyber maturity.
See this publication for further information on how adopting the TIBER-EU framework can help fulfil these DORA requirements.
TIBER-EU was developed jointly by the ECB and the EU’s national central banks, approved by the Governing Council of the ECB and published in May 2018."},{"url":"https://finreg.aoshearman.com/ECB-updates-TIBER-EU-framework-to-align-with-DORA","title":"A&O Shearman | FinReg | European Central Bank updates TIBER-EU framework to align with DORA RTS on TLPT","content":"The ECB also published a news item on the updated framework."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"ECB TIBER-EU framework DORA updated framework publication date cyber resilience 2025","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.regulationtomorrow.com/2025/11/cybersecurity-ecb-issues-tiber-eu-ssm-implementation-guide","title":"Cybersecurity – ECB issues TIBER-EU SSM Implementation Guide | Global Regulation Tomorrow","content":"On 21 November 2025, the European Central Bank (**ECB**) issued a guide that sets out how it adopts and implements the TIBER-EU framework for the threat-led penetration testing (**TLPT)** of significant institutions as identified for TLPT according to the Digital Operational Resilience Act (**DORA**). The ECB is the competent authority and TLPT authority (**TLPTA**) for significant institutions under Articles 26 and 46 of DORA and is thus ultimately responsible for the operationalisation of TLPT. The guide describes how the TIBER-EU framework is adopted and implemented by the ECB for the mandatory DORA TLPT of significant institutions as identified by the ECB. It provides guidance to significant institutions and all relevant stakeholders on the approach to be taken when carrying out the TLPT in practice, with the aim of helping fulfil the requirements under DORA and the regulatory technical standards on TLPT (Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2025/1190), while at the same time offering flexibility to adapt each test to the specific characteristics of the individual significant institution.","score":0.88420963,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.regulation-dora.eu/blog/tiber-eu-framework-dora-tlpt-testing-2025","title":"TIBER-EU Framework Updated for DORA: New TLPT Testing Requirements Explained | DORA Blog","content":"# TIBER-EU Framework Updated for DORA: New TLPT Testing Requirements Explained. TIBER-EU TLPT Penetration Testing Red Team Resilience Testing DORA 2025. In **February 2025**, the Eurosystem updated its European framework for threat intelligence-based ethical red-teaming (**TIBER-EU**) to align with DORA's regulatory technical standards on threat-led penetration testing (TLPT). This update provides comprehensive guidance for financial institutions planning their resilience testing programs. ## What is TIBER-EU? TIBER-EU is the European framework for **Threat Intelligence-Based Ethical Red-teaming**. It provides a standardized approach to testing the cyber resilience of financial institutions through controlled, intelligence-led attack simulations. * **Blue Team Response:** Testing of detection and response capabilities. ## DORA TLPT Requirements. Under DORA, certain financial entities must conduct **Threat-Led Penetration Testing (TLPT)** at least every three years. The updated TIBER-EU framework now fully aligns with these requirements. 2. **Threat intelligence:** Requirements for threat scenario development. ### Phase 2: Threat Intelligence (4-6 weeks). DORA and TIBER-EU specify requirements for TLPT providers:.","score":0.8827621,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.linkedin.com/posts/cyber-risk-gmbh_dora-update-11-february-2025-the-european-activity-7296085315981606912-otW1","title":"Cyber Risk GmbH's Post","content":"DORA UPDATE 11 February 2025 - The European framework for threat intelligence-based ethical red-teaming (TIBER-EU framework) is updated to","score":0.87681407,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.ecb.europa.eu/press/intro/news/html/ecb.mipnews250211.en.html","title":"TIBER-EU Framework updated to align with DORA","content":"Our monetary policy strategy, the tools we use and the impact they have. # TIBER-EU Framework updated to align with DORA. The Eurosystem has updated its European framework for threat intelligence-based ethical red-teaming (TIBER-EU framework), to align with the regulatory technical standards (RTS) of the Digital Operational Resilience Act (DORA) on threat-led penetration testing (TLPT). The TIBER-EU framework provides comprehensive guidance on how authorities, entities, and threat intelligence providers and red-team testers should work together to test and improve the cyber resilience of entities by carrying out controlled cyberattacks. The Eurosystem encourages authorities to adopt and implement the TIBER-EU framework, which now includes the required deliverables and steps to conduct threat intelligence-based red-team testing at designated financial entities in line with DORA requirements. The updated framework and the corresponding guidance documents are available on this webpage, where you can also find out more about the framework itself. For more information or to review your preference on the cookies and server logs we use, we invite you to:.","score":0.85421735,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://deepstrike.io/blog/tiber-eu-framework-explained","title":"TIBER-EU Explained: 2025 Framework & DORA TLPT","content":"# TIBER-EU Explained: Framework, Phases, Roles, and DORA TLPT Alignment. TIBER-EU is the ECB framework for controlled, intelligence-led red-team testing of Critical or Important Functions and the live systems that support them. This guide explains the January 2025 process, roles, evidence, safety controls and relationship with binding DORA TLPT requirements. TIBER-EU is the European Central Bank's framework for controlled, intelligence-led red-team testing of Critical or Important Functions and the live production systems supporting them. It is not EU law. The January 2025 framework is aligned with threat-led penetration testing under DORA, but for a mandatory test, DORA and Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2025/1190 define the binding requirements and take precedence. * TIBER-EU is a non-legally binding framework. DORA TLPT is a legal obligation only for financial entities identified under the applicable DORA process. The ECB describes it as a common European framework for authorities, tested entities, Threat Intelligence Providers and Red Team Testers to conduct controlled, intelligence-led attacks and improve cyber resilience.","score":0.8361405,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.88,"request_id":"9df21ea3-4905-4f0b-bb0f-a949727068e8"}
